title: Level 724
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 724: "The Forgotten News Studio"}}
{{Class 2|safety=Unsafe|security=Unstable|entity=Devoid of Entities}} == Description ==
[[File:Interior-Ford_Foundation-01.jpg|300px|alt=A garden with office windows, and a high glass roof.]]
One of Level 724's enclosed garden sets.
'''Level 724''', also known as "The Forgotten News Studio," is an old broadcasting studio that appears to have been built in the 1970s. Most of the level is made up of artificial outdoor-like sets enclosed inside a hall. At present, the level has no defined outer boundary. Researchers who walk in a straight line tend to cover the same areas repeatedly, or return to the central reception area. The layout alone does not make the level hazardous. Its main danger comes from broadcasts and documents that turn false reports into records of real people.
=== Artificial Exterior Sets ===
The artificial exterior sets are distinguished by artificial vegetation, such as bright green grass, planters, and artificial flowers. The main landmark is a semicircular reception desk made from smooth white material and located in the center of the largest set. This structure can be considered a landmark because many paths lead back to it. At the center of the reception desk, there is an old CRT computer with a distorted static gray image on it. The computer does not appear to be connected to anything. Lighting comes from light poles and fluorescent lamps distributed throughout the level. The ceiling is dark and rather high, measuring roughly 20 meters. The light poles and lamps provide illumination, giving the area an appearance similar to afternoon daylight.
=== Editing Rooms ===
There are editing rooms clustered together in one area. From the outside, they look like small white buildings located inside the artificial outdoor sets, and appear to be used as production and support facilities. In the editing rooms, there are radio receivers, display screens, and cameras. The screens and radio receivers do not react to commands. Documents scattered in the editing rooms begin as reports on public events that were never released as news. In close proximity to operating equipment, they begin to include personal details about people currently present in the rooms. A document related to Westbridge Elementary School in Editing Room 14 has been chosen for testing. It is unknown if these documents register changes, trigger changes, or only update alongside broadcasts.
== Anomalous Broadcasts ==
No physical entities have been documented in the level. The main danger is the broadcasting system itself. The broadcasts occur irregularly through radios, display screens in the editing rooms, and speakers spread across the level. While the screens may show a distorted image of the news channel most familiar to the listener, nearby radios may play static and distorted speech. Usually, each broadcast starts with a recognizable news tune or voiceover. At first, the broadcasts appear to give information about public events. Some of those events are real, but their details may be distorted. The location may remain the same, while the cause, date, or people involved may be replaced. The broadcast treats the version it presents as the real one.
In the first phase, some details of the event are changed. At that point, the listener can still feel that something is wrong with the report. The second phase begins when the listener is included in the event. People may remember attending this event even if it never took place or could not have involved them. The documents found near the active equipment may confirm this version. The third phase is connected with the records of the listener. Their names, photos, notes, and other documents begin to resemble the version offered by the broadcast more than the original identity. At that point, the broadcast is no longer treated as a lie, but becomes the truth accepted by the level. Some of the symptoms may lessen after leaving the active equipment in this level, but there is no guarantee that they will disappear. Some people affected by this level have recognized familiar faces but do not remember where they met them before. There is no certainty that exposure to the broadcasts of Level 724 will always affect someone permanently. Symptoms have faded in some early cases of exposure, especially if the person was not named or placed in the broadcast yet. However, once the broadcast starts replacing the records of the listener, there are no reports of the original identity being fully restored. This phenomenon cannot be explained by sound alone. The radios and screens in the editing rooms do not respond to normal controls, and muting them has not been proven to prevent the danger. Several experiments showed that muted screens continued to confuse people, displace memories, and change documents.
Recording 724-03 is the only known case in which the progression described above was recorded continuously.
== Entities ==
No physical entities have been documented in Level 724. The primary hazard of the level is its broadcasting system and its effect on memories, records, and personal identity.
== Broadcast Investigation Protocol ==
The following protocol was revised after Recording 724-03. It is not guaranteed to prevent identity loss, but it is currently the only procedure used for broadcast-related testing. # Do not investigate a broadcast alone. A second researcher must monitor the test remotely. # Before entering, write down your full name, one personal detail, and the purpose of the test. # Start recording before any equipment is activated. Do not stop the recording until the test has ended. # Leave immediately if the broadcast mentions your name, family, history, or any other personal detail. # If you have trouble recognizing your name or begin confusing your memories with the broadcast, the monitor must end the test and call a recovery team. # After leaving, compare your memories, notes, photographs, and recording for changes.
This protocol may delay identity erasure. It has not been proven to stop it.
== Items & Resources ==
Usable materials include blank paper, batteries, and recording devices that can be found in the editing rooms or locked in the storage cabinets. All of these materials are regarded as useful enough to conduct short studies, especially when it comes to writing names, recording memories, or documenting test conditions before turning on equipment. Some VHS tapes and newspaper clippings have also been found, although these are not considered reliable archival records due to their dangerous nature. Some VHS tapes contain partial broadcasts; however, symptoms have only been reported when these tapes are played. The effect is much weaker outside Level 724, but it is still noticeable. Therefore, any recovered tapes must be kept in sealed containers unless they are required for controlled experiments. Tapes are considered more dangerous than newspapers. Newspapers and other documents do not usually cause symptoms by themselves, but they may contain distorted names, false events, and information that is later referenced in broadcasts. Researchers are advised to photograph and compare such documents rather than rely on them. Almond Water is kept in very small amounts in the storage cabinet, although no reliable sources of food or water have been found yet. Researchers are not permitted to spend long periods of time in the level.
== Bases, Outposts and Communities == === M.E.G. Reception Monitoring Post ===
The M.E.G. Reception Monitoring Post is positioned next to the reception area, where it acts as a beacon for confused researchers. Within the post, one can find walkie-talkies, analog tape recorders, documents, name badges, and pictures of M.E.G. researchers. Before Recording 724-03, the post served as a small monitoring room for studies carried out in the adjoining editing rooms. The last known broadcast test done at the M.E.G. Reception Monitoring Post was carried out by Daniel Mercer and Liam Miller. Daniel worked from Editing Room 14, while Liam monitored him from the reception area using a walkie-talkie. After Daniel Mercer's disappearance, activity at the post became noticeably scarce. The event made it clear that remote monitoring and written records can also be influenced by the level. Some documents kept at the post contain names that no current M.E.G. researchers recognize.
== Entrances and Exits == === Entrances ===
Currently, there are two ways of accessing Level 724: # Entering Level 724 through a CRT monitor in [[Level 4]] that displays news broadcasts may bring a wanderer into one of the editing rooms. # The back entrance of a disused broadcasting office in [[Level 11]] may also provide access to Level 724.
=== Exits ===
There are two known exits out of the level: # On occasion, an elevator can be seen inside the wall of an editing room. Entering it and traveling down will result in appearing at a random place in [[Level 4]]. Afterwards, the elevator disappears and does not return to that room. # The reception doors usually open onto a dark wall. However, when an empty street lies ahead of them, passing through those doors will result in appearing in a random office building in [[Level 11]].
== Recovered Broadcast ==
=== Recovered Recording 724-03 ===
[[File:Level724_editing_room_archival.png|300px|alt=A video-editing room containing several CRT monitors, recording equipment, control panels, and old devices.]]
Equipment similar to that used in various editing rooms.
'''Source:''' Editing Room 14
'''Recorder:''' Researcher Daniel Mercer
'''Monitor:''' Researcher Liam Miller
'''Status:''' Unconfirmed source; named references should not be edited. Recording 724-03 is the incident that led to the current broadcast investigation protocol. Before this test, Level 724's broadcasts were treated mainly as memory hazards. The loss of Daniel Mercer showed that the level could also alter records, monitoring notes, and the memories of people who were not physically inside the active room. Room 14 was selected for the study when the team found an article about flooding at Westbridge Elementary School. Daniel Mercer, who had studied at that school, was the only team member able to identify the report as false.
[[File:school_newspaper.jpg|250px|alt=A cropped old newspaper clipping about Westbridge Elementary School closing after flooding.]]
The Westbridge article recovered from Room 14.
The goal of the experiment was to find out whether an external recording could remain stable while the broadcast changed the perception or records of the listener. Daniel believed that if his own memory changed, the recording might still show what really happened. Before starting the procedure, Liam and Daniel formed an anchor: Daniel's name, his father's name, and the long-standing friendship between the two researchers.
M.E.G. ARCHIVE
[[File:Meg.png|90px|alt=M.E.G. group icon]]
FILE 724-03
Recovered Recording 724-03
Recovered from Editing Room 14 after Daniel Mercer disappeared. The transcript was copied from the recorder cache and kept in order.

Source: Editing Room 14

Recorder: Daniel Mercer

Monitor: Liam Miller

Status: Unconfirmed source. Do not edit named references.

Transcript
[00:00]

Daniel: I’m in Room 14. Liam’s monitoring me from reception through the radio. As soon as my name comes up in the broadcast, I’ll leave.

Liam: First make sure your anchor is secured before switching on the device.

Daniel: I am Daniel Mercer, son of James Mercer, and I’m here to investigate the Westbridge document.

Liam: Okay, now record yourself and switch on the device.

Daniel: Recording has begun. Device is on. I see a news broadcast on the screen. All right, so far, everything looks normal.

[01:42]

Broadcast: The authorities have officially confirmed that Westbridge Elementary School was shut down due to flooding.

Daniel: I’ve heard about that school. I went to that school. I know there was no flooding at all. The school was closed much later than that and certainly not due to any flooding.

Liam: Okay, that’s enough. Back away from the equipment, now!

Daniel: They haven’t named me yet.

[03:18]

Broadcast: One of the missing Westbridge students is Daniel Mercer.

Liam: Your name has come up. Leave the room, Daniel.

Daniel: But I must find out if the tape still tells the truth or not.

Liam: That wasn't the plan. You already crossed the line!

Daniel: If I leave now, all we know is that it can say my name.

[04:51]

Broadcast: According to Mercer's family members, Daniel experienced some memory problems a week prior to disappearing.

Daniel: I don't remember the house number. I should know it, but I don't. I definitely recorded that information in my notes somewhere.

Liam: Read your card. Don’t answer it!

Daniel: I am Daniel Mercer. My father is James Mercer. I entered Room 14 to check the document on Westbridge.

[05:28]

Daniel: I can still read it. I don’t think it feels real anymore.

[06:07]

Broadcast: The search is over. There is no picture of the missing man.

Liam: Daniel, your name is Daniel Mercer. Your father’s name is James Mercer. We lived next to each other as children. You used to wait outside my house before school because my mother believed we had to get ready in advance.

Daniel: Who are you referring to, Liam? Are there any other people here in the room?

Liam: No, I’m talking to you, Daniel. Just read the anchor.

Daniel: Yes, I can hear you, but who are you?

[07:26]

Broadcast: And that's the end of today's broadcast.

[The screen now shows an empty editing room. No researcher can be spotted anywhere in the room.]

Daniel: Can you repeat the name?

[Recording ended.]
== Post-Incident Note == The recovery team was able to retrieve the recorder belonging to Daniel Mercer, which had been left on a chair in the editing room. Daniel Mercer's whereabouts are currently unknown.
[[File:Level724_Daniel_Mercer_ID.png|360px|alt=A Major Explorer Group ID card for Daniel Mercer, marked Broadcast Test 724-03 with the photo field blank.]]
A damaged copy of Daniel Mercer's M.E.G. ID. The photograph field was blank when recovered.
The mission report confirmed that the test did take place and contained all necessary information except for one thing: Daniel Mercer himself. Daniel's name, picture, and other personal information were removed from the mission report, as well as the printed records stored at the reception post. Recording 724-03 is the only piece of evidence left of Daniel Mercer's presence in Room 14. Liam Miller's voice can be heard talking to him throughout the recording; however, Liam himself says that he does not remember ever working with Daniel or observing the test. After being asked to recognize Daniel's voice, Liam stated that he recognized it but did not know why. Because of this incident, all broadcast tests conducted in Level 724 are stopped once any personal information about the researcher is mentioned in the broadcast. It is currently unknown whether this prevents further deletions or only delays them.
